Rutherford, secretary and general manager of the National Benefit Life Insurance Company, announces receipt of information of the disposition of the criminal proceedings pending since June, against P. He was also charged and pleaded guilty to the charge of "larceny" by trick, in that he obtained $650 by fraudulent claim of George Smith, by succeeding in having a Fort Worth physician certify that Smith was dead, and securing a death certificate, a permit issued, authorizing the undertaker's interment of Smith's body. During this time, Smith was employed by the City Gas Company of El Paso, Texas—some three hundred miles away—and knew nothing about the alleged death and interment.
